revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''金刀比羅・大鷲神社'''（ことひら・おおとりじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[南区 (横浜市)|南区]][[真金町]]にあ...'
revision.text:
'''金刀比羅・大鷲神社'''（ことひら・おおとりじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[南区 (横浜市)|南区]][[真金町]]にある[[神社]]である。旧無格社。境内末社に[[伏見稲荷神社]]がある。 ==祭神== *金刀比羅神社 **[[大物主|大物主命]] **[[崇徳天皇]] *大鷲神社 **[[鳥之石楠船神|天之鳥船命]] ==歴史== *金刀比羅神社 [[1859年]]横浜開港にあたり、[[港崎遊郭]]の岩亀楼主人である岩槻屋佐吉が[[讃岐国]][[金刀比羅宮|象頭山 金比羅大権現]]を[[勧請]]し、港崎町（現在の[[横浜公園]]の地）に祭祀した。
[[1872年]][[高島 (横浜市)|高島町7丁目]]の海側に遷座。[[遊廓]]の移転によるものであった。[[1882年]]4月同様の理由で真金町に遷座。
[[1923年]][[9月1日]]の[[関東大震災]]と[[1945年]][[5月29日]]の[[横浜大空襲]]で共に被災。その度に再建されたが、現在の社殿は[[1988年]]8月に更に再建されたもの。 *大鷲神社 1872年港崎遊廓が高島町に移転した際、[[東京]]の[[吉原遊廓|吉原]]に倣い、「おとり様」を勧請した東京[[浅草]]の[[鷲神社 (台東区)|鷲神社]]なのか、その本筋である[[大鷲神社 (足立区)|大鷲神社]]なのかは明確でない。。この時より、浅草と同様に酉の市が行われるようになった。当初は境内の末社であり別宮となっていたが、現在は金刀比羅神社の相殿に祀られている。 ==所在地== *横浜市南区真金町1-3 ==脚注== ==関連項目== *[[酉の市]] {{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらおおとりしんしや}} [[category:横浜市の神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''金刀比羅・大鷲神社'''（ことひら・おおとりじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[南区 (横浜市)|南区]][[真金町]]にある[[神社]]である。旧無格社。境内末社に[[伏見稲荷神社]]がある。 ==祭神== *金刀比羅神社 **[[大物主|大物主命]] **[[崇徳天皇]] *大鷲神社 **[[鳥之石楠船神|天之鳥船命]] ==歴史== '''金刀比羅神社'''
:[[1859年]]横浜開港にあたり、[[港崎遊郭]]の岩亀楼主人である岩槻屋佐吉が[[讃岐国]][[金刀比羅宮|象頭山 金比羅大権現]]を[[勧請]]し、港崎町（現在の[[横浜公園]]の地）に祭祀した。
[[1872年]][[高島 (横浜市)|高島町7丁目]]の海側に遷座。[[遊廓]]の移転によるものであった。[[1882年]]4月同様の理由で真金町に遷座。
[[1923年]][[9月1日]]の[[関東大震災]]と[[1945年]][[5月29日]]の[[横浜大空襲]]で共に被災。その度に再建されたが、現在の社殿は[[1988年]]8月に更に再建されたもの。
'''大鷲神社'''
:1872年港崎遊廓が高島町に移転した際、[[東京]]の[[吉原遊廓|吉原]]に倣い、「おとり様」を勧請した東京[[浅草]]の[[鷲神社 (台東区)|鷲神社]]なのか、その本筋である東京足立花畑の[[大鷲神社 (足立区)|大鷲神社]]なのかは明確でない。。この時より、浅草と同様に酉の市が行われるようになった。当初は境内の末社であり別宮となっていたが、現在は金刀比羅神社の相殿に祀られている。 ==所在地== *横浜市南区真金町1-3 ==脚注== ==関連項目== *[[酉の市]] {{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらおおとりしんしや}} [[category:横浜市の神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''金刀比羅・大鷲神社'''（ことひら・おおとりじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[南区 (横浜市)|南区]][[真金町]]にある[[神社]]である。旧無格社。境内末社に[[伏見稲荷神社]]がある。 ==祭神== *金刀比羅神社 **[[大物主|大物主命]] **[[崇徳天皇]] *大鷲神社 **[[鳥之石楠船神|天之鳥船命]] ==歴史== '''金刀比羅神社'''

[[1872年]][[高島 (横浜市)|高島町7丁目]]の海側に遷座。[[遊廓]]の移転によるものであった。[[1882年]]4月同様の理由で真金町に遷座。
[[1923年]][[9月1日]]の[[関東大震災]]と[[1945年]][[5月29日]]の[[横浜大空襲]]で共に被災。その度に再建されたが、現在の社殿は[[1988年]]8月に更に再建されたもの。

鳥居と拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|26|13|N|139|37|43|E}}}} |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[南区 (横浜市)|南区]][[真金町]]1-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|26|13|N|139|37|43|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅・大鷲神社}} |祭神 = [[大物主命]]、[[崇徳天皇]]、[[鳥之石楠船神|天之鳥船命]] |社格 = 無格社 |創建 = 1859年 |本殿 = |別名 = 金刀比羅・大鷲神社 |例祭 = |神事 = |}} '''金刀比羅・大鷲神社'''（ことひら・おおとりじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[南区 (横浜市)|南区]][[真金町]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[近代社格制度#無格社|無格社]]。[[法人登記|登記]]上の[[宗教法人]]名称は'''宗教法人金刀比羅神社'''（しゅうきょうほうじんことひらじんじゃ）。境内末社に[[伏見稲荷神社]]がある。 ==祭神== *金刀比羅神社 **[[大物主|大物主命]] **[[崇徳天皇]] *大鷲神社 **[[鳥之石楠船神|天之鳥船命]] ==歴史== ;金刀比羅神社 :[[1859年]]横浜開港にあたり、[[港崎遊郭]]の岩亀楼主人である岩槻屋佐吉が[[讃岐国]][[金刀比羅宮|象頭山 金毘羅大権現]]を[[勧請]]し、港崎町（現在の[[横浜公園]]の地）に祭祀した。
[[1872年]]に[[遊廓]]の移転とともに[[高島 (横浜市)|高島町7丁目]]の海側へ遷座し金刀比羅神社と改称した。[[1882年]]4月に同様の理由で真金町へ遷座した。
[[1923年]][[9月1日]]の[[関東大震災]]と[[1945年]][[5月29日]]の[[横浜大空襲]]で共に被災。その度に再建されたが、現在の社殿は[[1988年]]8月に更に再建されたもの。 ;大鷲神社 :1872年港崎遊廓が高島町に移転した際、[[東京]]の[[吉原遊廓|吉原]]に倣い、「おとり様」を勧請した東京[[浅草]]の[[鷲神社 (台東区)|鷲神社]]なのか、その本筋である東京足立花畑の[[大鷲神社 (足立区)|大鷲神社]]なのかは明確でない。。この時より、浅草と同様に[[酉の市]]が行われるようになった。当初は境内の末社であり別宮となっていたが、現在は金刀比羅神社の相殿に祀られている。 ==所在地== *神奈川県横浜市南区真金町1-3 ==脚注== ==関連項目== *[[酉の市]] ==外部リンク== *[http://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdo.com/ 金刀比羅大鷲神社] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらおおとりしんしや}} [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:横浜市南区の建築物]] [[Category:横浜市南区の歴史]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧無格社]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]Created page with '{{draft}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} located in {{ill|Japan|simple}}. It worships {{ill|Konpira Gongen|simple}}. {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} are [[shinto shrine]]s dedicated to [[Ōmononushi]]. Due to [[Shinbutsu-shūgō]] they often also worship [[Konpira Gongen]] They are part of the famous Inahachikonten Shrines (稲八金天神社) of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]]...'

{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅神社 |画像 = [[File:Kotohira & Ōtori Jinjja.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|26|13|N|139|37|43|E}}}} |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[南区 (横浜市)|南区]][[真金町]]1-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|26|13|N|139|37|43|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅・大鷲神社}} |祭神 = [[大物主命]]、[[崇徳天皇]]、[[鳥之石楠船神|天之鳥船命]] |社格 = 無格社 |創建 = [[1859年]]（[[安政]]6年） |本殿 = |別名 = 金刀比羅・大鷲神社 |例祭 = |神事 = |}} '''金刀比羅・大鷲神社'''（ことひら・おおとりじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[南区 (横浜市)|南区]][[真金町]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[近代社格制度#無格社|無格社]]。[[法人登記|登記]]上の[[宗教法人]]名称は'''宗教法人金刀比羅神社'''（しゅうきょうほうじんことひらじんじゃ）。境内末社に[[伏見稲荷神社]]がある。 ==祭神== *金刀比羅神社 **[[大物主|大物主命]] **[[崇徳天皇]] *大鷲神社 **[[鳥之石楠船神|天之鳥船命]] ==歴史== ;金刀比羅神社 :[[1859年]]（[[安政]]6年）、[[横浜港]]開港にあたり、[[港崎遊郭]]の岩亀楼主人である岩槻屋佐吉が[[讃岐国]][[金刀比羅宮|象頭山 金毘羅大権現]]を[[勧請]]し、港崎町（現在の[[横浜公園]]の地）に祭祀した。
[[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）に[[遊廓]]の移転とともに[[高島 (横浜市)|高島町7丁目]]の海側へ遷座し金刀比羅神社と改称した。[[1882年]]（明治15年）4月に同様の理由で真金町へ遷座した。
[[1923年]]（[[大正]]12年）[[9月1日]]の[[関東大震災]]と[[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）[[5月29日]]の[[横浜大空襲]]で共に被災。その度に再建されたが、現在の社殿は[[1988年]]（昭和63年）8月に更に再建されたもの。 ;大鷲神社 :[[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）、港崎遊廓が高島町に移転した際、[[東京]]の[[吉原遊廓|吉原]]に倣い、「おとり様」を勧請した東京[[浅草]]の[[鷲神社 (台東区)|鷲神社]]なのか、その本筋である東京足立花畑の[[大鷲神社 (足立区)|大鷲神社]]なのかは明確でない。。この時より、浅草と同様に[[酉の市]]が行われるようになった。当初は境内の末社であり別宮となっていたが、現在は金刀比羅神社の相殿に祀られている。 ==所在地== *神奈川県横浜市南区真金町1-3 ==脚注== ==関連項目== *[[酉の市]] ==外部リンク== *[https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ 金刀比羅大鷲神社] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらおおとりしんしや}} [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:横浜市南区の建築物]] [[Category:横浜市南区の歴史]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧無格社]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: Created page with "{{draft}} [[File:Kotohira & Ōtori Jinjja.jpg|thumb]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] {{ill|Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社}} is a {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} located in {{ill|Japan|simple}}. It worships {{ill|Konpira Gongen|simple}}. {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} are [[shinto shrine]]s dedicated to [[Ōmononushi]]. Due to [[Shinbutsu-shūgō]] they often also worship [[Konpira Gongen]]..."

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | image = [[File:Kotohira & Ōtori Jinjja.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|26|13|N|139|37|43|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Minami-ku (Yokohama)|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}, {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} 1-3 | coordinates = {{ill|Coordinates|ja|ウィキ座標}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{Interlanguage link multi|Ame-no-torifune|ja|3=鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup}} | shrine_rank = Former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}} | established = {{ill|1859|ja|1859年}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年}}) | main_hall = | other_names = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | annual_festival = | rituals = }} '''Kotohira Ōtori Shrine''' (金刀比羅・大鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}}, {{ill|Minami-ku (Yokohama)|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} ** {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} * Ōtori Shrine ** {{Interlanguage link multi|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup}} == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In {{ill|1859|ja|1859年}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年}}), with the opening of {{ill|Yokohama Port|ja|横浜港}}, the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園}}).
In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), along with the relocation of the {{ill|Yūkaku|ja|遊廓}}, the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In {{ill|1882|ja|1882年}} ({{ill|Meiji 15|ja|明治15年}}), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} on September 1, {{ill|1923|ja|1923年}} ({{ill|Taishō 12|ja|大正12年}}) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲}} on May 29, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 20|ja|昭和20年}}). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 63|ja|昭和63年}}). ; Ōtori Shrine In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrines]] [[Category:Former unranked shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{draft categories| {{ill|Category:Kotohira shrines|ja|Category:金刀比羅神社}} [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:Unsorted link categories]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump1-19]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), along with the relocation of the {{ill|Yūkaku|ja|遊廓}}, the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In {{ill|1882|ja|1882年}} ({{ill|Meiji 15|ja|明治15年}}), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} on September 1, {{ill|1923|ja|1923年}} ({{ill|Taishō 12|ja|大正12年}}) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲}} on May 29, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 20|ja|昭和20年}}). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 63|ja|昭和63年}}). ; Ōtori Shrine In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrines]] [[Category:Former unranked shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{draft categories| {{ill|Category:Kotohira shrines|ja|Category:金刀比羅神社}} [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:Unsorted link categories]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrines]] [[Category:Former unranked shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{draft categories| {{ill|Category:Kotohira shrines|ja|Category:金刀比羅神社}} [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

{{Shrine | name = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | image = [[File:Kotohira & Ōtori Jinjja.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|26|13|N|139|37|43|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Minami-ku (Yokohama)|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}, {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} 1-3 | coordinates = {{ill|Coordinates|ja|ウィキ座標}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{Interlanguage link multi|Ame-no-torifune|ja|3=鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup}} | shrine_rank = Former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}} | established = {{ill|1859|ja|1859年}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年}}) | main_hall = | other_names = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | annual_festival = | rituals = }} '''Kotohira Ōtori Shrine''' (金刀比羅・大鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}}, {{ill|Minami-ku (Yokohama)|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} ** {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} * Ōtori Shrine ** {{Interlanguage link multi|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup}} == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In {{ill|1859|ja|1859年}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年}}), with the opening of {{ill|Yokohama Port|ja|横浜港}}, the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園}}).
In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), along with the relocation of the {{ill|Yūkaku|ja|遊廓}}, the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In {{ill|1882|ja|1882年}} ({{ill|Meiji 15|ja|明治15年}}), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} on September 1, {{ill|1923|ja|1923年}} ({{ill|Taishō 12|ja|大正12年}}) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲}} on May 29, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 20|ja|昭和20年}}). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 63|ja|昭和63年}}). ; Ōtori Shrine In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrines]] [[Category:Former unranked shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{draft categories| {{ill|Category:Kotohira shrines|ja|Category:金刀比羅神社}} [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Images]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Kotohira & Ōtori Shrine]] to [[Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | image = [[File:Kotohira & Ōtori Jinjja.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|26|13|N|139|37|43|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Minami-ku (Yokohama)|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}, {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} 1-3 | coordinates = {{ill|Coordinates|ja|ウィキ座標}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{Interlanguage link multi|Ame-no-torifune|ja|3=鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup}} | shrine_rank = Former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}} | established = {{ill|1859|ja|1859年}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年}}) | main_hall = | other_names = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | annual_festival = | rituals = }} '''Kotohira Ōtori Shrine''' (金刀比羅・大鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}}, {{ill|Minami-ku (Yokohama)|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} ** {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} * Ōtori Shrine ** {{Interlanguage link multi|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup}} == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In {{ill|1859|ja|1859年}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年}}), with the opening of {{ill|Yokohama Port|ja|横浜港}}, the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園}}).
In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), along with the relocation of the {{ill|Yūkaku|ja|遊廓}}, the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In {{ill|1882|ja|1882年}} ({{ill|Meiji 15|ja|明治15年}}), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} on September 1, {{ill|1923|ja|1923年}} ({{ill|Taishō 12|ja|大正12年}}) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲}} on May 29, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 20|ja|昭和20年}}). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 63|ja|昭和63年}}). ; Ōtori Shrine In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrines]] [[Category:Former unranked shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{draft categories| {{ill|Category:Kotohira shrines|ja|Category:金刀比羅神社}} [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Images]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|26|13|N|139|37|43|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Minami-ku (Yokohama)|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}, {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} 1-3 | coordinates = {{ill|Coordinates|ja|ウィキ座標}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{Interlanguage link multi|Ame-no-torifune|ja|3=鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup}} | shrine_rank = Former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}} | established = {{ill|1859|ja|1859年}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年}}) | main_hall = | other_names = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | annual_festival = | rituals = }} '''Kotohira Ōtori Shrine''' (金刀比羅・大鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}}, {{ill|Minami-ku (Yokohama)|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} ** {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} * Ōtori Shrine ** {{Interlanguage link multi|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup}} == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In {{ill|1859|ja|1859年}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年}}), with the opening of {{ill|Yokohama Port|ja|横浜港}}, the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園}}).
In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), along with the relocation of the {{ill|Yūkaku|ja|遊廓}}, the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In {{ill|1882|ja|1882年}} ({{ill|Meiji 15|ja|明治15年}}), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} on September 1, {{ill|1923|ja|1923年}} ({{ill|Taishō 12|ja|大正12年}}) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲}} on May 29, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 20|ja|昭和20年}}). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 63|ja|昭和63年}}). ; Ōtori Shrine In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Former unranked shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{draft categories| {{ill|Category:Kotohira shrines|ja|Category:金刀比羅神社}} [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Images]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|26|13|N|139|37|43|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Minami-ku (Yokohama)|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}, {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} 1-3 | coordinates = {{ill|coordinates|ja|ウィキ座標}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{Interlanguage link multi|Ame-no-torifune|ja|3=鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup}} | shrine_rank = Former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}} | established = {{ill|1859|ja|1859年}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年}}) | main_hall = | other_names = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | annual_festival = | rituals = }} '''Kotohira Ōtori Shrine''' (金刀比羅・大鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}}, {{ill|Minami-ku (Yokohama)|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} ** {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} * Ōtori Shrine ** {{Interlanguage link multi|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup}} == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In {{ill|1859|ja|1859年}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年}}), with the opening of {{ill|Yokohama Port|ja|横浜港}}, the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園}}).
In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), along with the relocation of the {{ill|Yūkaku|ja|遊廓}}, the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In {{ill|1882|ja|1882年}} ({{ill|Meiji 15|ja|明治15年}}), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} on September 1, {{ill|1923|ja|1923年}} ({{ill|Taishō 12|ja|大正12年}}) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲}} on May 29, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 20|ja|昭和20年}}). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 63|ja|昭和63年}}). ; Ōtori Shrine In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Former unranked shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{draft categories| {{ill|Category:Kotohira shrines|ja|Category:金刀比羅神社}} [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:images]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

{{Shrine | name = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohira & Ōtori Jinjja.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|26|13|N|139|37|43|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Minami-ku (Yokohama)|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}, {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} 1-3 | coordinates = {{ill|coordinates|ja|ウィキ座標}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{Interlanguage link multi|Ame-no-torifune|ja|3=鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup}} | shrine_rank = Former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}} | established = {{ill|1859|ja|1859年}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年}}) | main_hall = | other_names = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | annual_festival = | rituals = }} '''Kotohira Ōtori Shrine''' (金刀比羅・大鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}}, {{ill|Minami-ku (Yokohama)|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} ** {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} * Ōtori Shrine ** {{Interlanguage link multi|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup}} == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In {{ill|1859|ja|1859年}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年}}), with the opening of {{ill|Yokohama Port|ja|横浜港}}, the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園}}).
In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), along with the relocation of the {{ill|Yūkaku|ja|遊廓}}, the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In {{ill|1882|ja|1882年}} ({{ill|Meiji 15|ja|明治15年}}), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} on September 1, {{ill|1923|ja|1923年}} ({{ill|Taishō 12|ja|大正12年}}) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲}} on May 29, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 20|ja|昭和20年}}). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 63|ja|昭和63年}}). ; Ōtori Shrine In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohira & Ōtori Jinjja.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|26|13|N|139|37|43|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Minami-ku (Yokohama)|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}, {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} 1-3 | coordinates = {{ill|coordinates|ja|ウィキ座標}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{Interlanguage link multi|Ame-no-torifune|ja|3=鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup}} | shrine_rank = Former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}} | established = {{ill|1859|ja|1859年}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年}}) | main_hall = | other_names = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | annual_festival = | rituals = }} '''Kotohira Ōtori Shrine''' (金刀比羅・大鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}}, {{ill|Minami-ku (Yokohama)|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} ** {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} * Ōtori Shrine ** {{Interlanguage link multi|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup}} == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In {{ill|1859|ja|1859年}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年}}), with the opening of {{ill|Yokohama Port|ja|横浜港}}, the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園}}).
In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), along with the relocation of the {{ill|Yūkaku|ja|遊廓}}, the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In {{ill|1882|ja|1882年}} ({{ill|Meiji 15|ja|明治15年}}), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} on September 1, {{ill|1923|ja|1923年}} ({{ill|Taishō 12|ja|大正12年}}) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲}} on May 29, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 20|ja|昭和20年}}). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 63|ja|昭和63年}}). ; Ōtori Shrine In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Former unranked shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{draft categories| {{ill|Category:Kotohira shrines|ja|Category:金刀比羅神社}} [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:kk]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} on September 1, {{ill|1923|ja|1923年}} ({{ill|Taishō 12|ja|大正12年}}) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲}} on May 29, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 20|ja|昭和20年}}). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 63|ja|昭和63年}}). ; Ōtori Shrine In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Former unranked shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{draft categories| {{ill|Category:Kotohira shrines|ja|Category:金刀比羅神社}} [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Otori Faith}}

{{Shrine | name = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohira & Ōtori Jinjja.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|26|13|N|139|37|43|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Minami-ku (Yokohama)|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}, {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} 1-3 | coordinates = {{ill|coordinates|ja|ウィキ座標}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{Interlanguage link multi|Ame-no-torifune|ja|3=鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup}} | shrine_rank = Former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}} | established = {{ill|1859|ja|1859年}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年}}) | main_hall = | other_names = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | annual_festival = | rituals = }} '''Kotohira Ōtori Shrine''' (金刀比羅・大鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}}, {{ill|Minami-ku (Yokohama)|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} ** {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} * Ōtori Shrine ** {{Interlanguage link multi|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup}} == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In {{ill|1859|ja|1859年}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年}}), with the opening of {{ill|Yokohama Port|ja|横浜港}}, the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園}}).
In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), along with the relocation of the {{ill|Yūkaku|ja|遊廓}}, the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In {{ill|1882|ja|1882年}} ({{ill|Meiji 15|ja|明治15年}}), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} on September 1, {{ill|1923|ja|1923年}} ({{ill|Taishō 12|ja|大正12年}}) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲}} on May 29, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 20|ja|昭和20年}}). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 63|ja|昭和63年}}). ; Ōtori Shrine In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Former unranked shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{draft categories| {{ill|Category:Kotohira shrines|ja|Category:金刀比羅神社}} [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]]}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Otori Faith}}

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=original page at [[every:Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]][[en:draft:Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]][[:en:draft:Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:35, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohira & Ōtori Jinjja.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|26|13|N|139|37|43|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Minami-ku (Yokohama)|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}, {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} 1-3 | coordinates = {{ill|coordinates|ja|ウィキ座標}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{Interlanguage link multi|Ame-no-torifune|ja|3=鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup}} | shrine_rank = Former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}} | established = {{ill|1859|ja|1859年}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年}}) | main_hall = | other_names = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | annual_festival = | rituals = }} '''Kotohira Ōtori Shrine''' (金刀比羅・大鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}}, {{ill|Minami-ku (Yokohama)|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} ** {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} * Ōtori Shrine ** {{Interlanguage link multi|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup}} == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In {{ill|1859|ja|1859年}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年}}), with the opening of {{ill|Yokohama Port|ja|横浜港}}, the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園}}).
In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), along with the relocation of the {{ill|Yūkaku|ja|遊廓}}, the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In {{ill|1882|ja|1882年}} ({{ill|Meiji 15|ja|明治15年}}), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} on September 1, {{ill|1923|ja|1923年}} ({{ill|Taishō 12|ja|大正12年}}) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲}} on May 29, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 20|ja|昭和20年}}). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 63|ja|昭和63年}}). ; Ōtori Shrine In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Former unranked shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{draft categories| {{ill|Category:Kotohira shrines|ja|Category:金刀比羅神社}} [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:Everybodywiki manual marking]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Otori Faith}} [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:横浜市南区の建築物]] [[Category:横浜市南区の歴史]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧無格社]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages, need dummy edit imported for attriubtion, if they are redirects the redirects need to be reviewed, adding jawiki link which must be reviewed to see if the page is already present here. Later on the categories should be renamed too.]][[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Mass imported shinto cult pages, need dummy edit imported for attriubtion, if they are redirects the redirects need to be reviewed, adding jawiki link which must be reviewed to see if the page is already present here. Later on the categories should be renamed too]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{shinto-stub}}

It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=original page at [[every:Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]][[en:draft:Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]][[:en:draft:Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:35, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohira & Ōtori Jinjja.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|26|13|N|139|37|43|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Minami-ku (Yokohama)|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}, {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} 1-3 | coordinates = {{ill|coordinates|ja|ウィキ座標}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{Interlanguage link multi|Ame-no-torifune|ja|3=鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup}} | shrine_rank = Former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}} | established = {{ill|1859|ja|1859年}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年}}) | main_hall = | other_names = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | annual_festival = | rituals = }} '''Kotohira Ōtori Shrine''' (金刀比羅・大鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}}, {{ill|Minami-ku (Yokohama)|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} ** {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} * Ōtori Shrine ** {{Interlanguage link multi|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup}} == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In {{ill|1859|ja|1859年}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年}}), with the opening of {{ill|Yokohama Port|ja|横浜港}}, the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園}}).
In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), along with the relocation of the {{ill|Yūkaku|ja|遊廓}}, the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In {{ill|1882|ja|1882年}} ({{ill|Meiji 15|ja|明治15年}}), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} on September 1, {{ill|1923|ja|1923年}} ({{ill|Taishō 12|ja|大正12年}}) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲}} on May 29, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 20|ja|昭和20年}}). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 63|ja|昭和63年}}). ; Ōtori Shrine In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Former unranked shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{draft categories| {{ill|Category:Kotohira shrines|ja|Category:金刀比羅神社}} [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:Everybodywiki manual marking]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Otori Faith}} [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:横浜市南区の建築物]] [[Category:横浜市南区の歴史]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧無格社]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Mass imported shinto cult pages, need dummy edit imported for attriubtion, if they are redirects the redirects need to be reviewed, adding jawiki link which must be reviewed to see if the page is already present here. Later on the categories should be renamed too]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{shinto-stub}}[[Category:Pages needing attribution]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|26|13|N|139|37|43|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Minami-ku (Yokohama)|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}, {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} 1-3 | coordinates = {{ill|coordinates|ja|ウィキ座標}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{Interlanguage link multi|Ame-no-torifune|ja|3=鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup}} | shrine_rank = Former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}} | established = {{ill|1859|ja|1859年}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年}}) | main_hall = | other_names = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | annual_festival = | rituals = }} '''Kotohira Ōtori Shrine''' (金刀比羅・大鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}}, {{ill|Minami-ku (Yokohama)|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} ** {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} * Ōtori Shrine ** {{Interlanguage link multi|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup}} == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In {{ill|1859|ja|1859年}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年}}), with the opening of {{ill|Yokohama Port|ja|横浜港}}, the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園}}).
In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), along with the relocation of the {{ill|Yūkaku|ja|遊廓}}, the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In {{ill|1882|ja|1882年}} ({{ill|Meiji 15|ja|明治15年}}), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} on September 1, {{ill|1923|ja|1923年}} ({{ill|Taishō 12|ja|大正12年}}) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲}} on May 29, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 20|ja|昭和20年}}). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 63|ja|昭和63年}}). ; Ōtori Shrine In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Former unranked shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{draft categories| {{ill|Category:Kotohira shrines|ja|Category:金刀比羅神社}} [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:Everybodywiki manual marking]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Otori Faith}} [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:横浜市南区の建築物]] [[Category:横浜市南区の歴史]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧無格社]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Mass imported shinto cult pages, need dummy edit imported for attriubtion, if they are redirects the redirects need to be reviewed, adding jawiki link which must be reviewed to see if the page is already present here. Later on the categories should be renamed too]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{shinto-stub}}[[Category:Pages needing attribution]]

Torii and Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|26|13|N|139|37|43|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Minami-ku (Yokohama)|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}, {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} 1-3 | coordinates = {{ill|coordinates|ja|ウィキ座標}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{Interlanguage link multi|Ame-no-torifune|ja|3=鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup}} | shrine_rank = Former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}} | established = {{ill|1859|ja|1859年}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年}}) | main_hall = | other_names = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | annual_festival = | rituals = }} '''Kotohira Ōtori Shrine''' (金刀比羅・大鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}}, {{ill|Minami-ku (Yokohama)|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} ** {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} * Ōtori Shrine ** {{Interlanguage link multi|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup}} == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In {{ill|1859|ja|1859年}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年}}), with the opening of {{ill|Yokohama Port|ja|横浜港}}, the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園}}).
In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), along with the relocation of the {{ill|Yūkaku|ja|遊廓}}, the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In {{ill|1882|ja|1882年}} ({{ill|Meiji 15|ja|明治15年}}), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} on September 1, {{ill|1923|ja|1923年}} ({{ill|Taishō 12|ja|大正12年}}) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲}} on May 29, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 20|ja|昭和20年}}). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 63|ja|昭和63年}}). ; Ōtori Shrine In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} Torii and Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|26|13|N|139|37|43|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Minami-ku (Yokohama)|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}, {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} 1-3 | coordinates = {{ill|coordinates|ja|ウィキ座標}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{Interlanguage link multi|Ame-no-torifune|ja|3=鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup}} | shrine_rank = Former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}} | established = {{ill|1859|ja|1859年}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年}}) | main_hall = | other_names = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | annual_festival = | rituals = }} '''Kotohira Ōtori Shrine''' (金刀比羅・大鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}}, {{ill|Minami-ku (Yokohama)|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} ** {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} * Ōtori Shrine ** {{Interlanguage link multi|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup}} == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In {{ill|1859|ja|1859年}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年}}), with the opening of {{ill|Yokohama Port|ja|横浜港}}, the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園}}).
In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), along with the relocation of the {{ill|Yūkaku|ja|遊廓}}, the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In {{ill|1882|ja|1882年}} ({{ill|Meiji 15|ja|明治15年}}), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} on September 1, {{ill|1923|ja|1923年}} ({{ill|Taishō 12|ja|大正12年}}) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲}} on May 29, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 20|ja|昭和20年}}). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 63|ja|昭和63年}}). ; Ōtori Shrine In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} Torii and Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|26|13|N|139|37|43|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Minami-ku (Yokohama)|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}, {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} 1-3 | coordinates = {{ill|coordinates|ja|ウィキ座標}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{Interlanguage link multi|Ame-no-torifune|ja|3=鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup}} | shrine_rank = Former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}} | established = {{ill|1859|ja|1859年}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年}}) | main_hall = | other_names = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | annual_festival = | rituals = }} '''Kotohira Ōtori Shrine''' (金刀比羅・大鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}}, {{ill|Minami-ku (Yokohama)|ja|南区 (横浜市)}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} ** {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} * Ōtori Shrine ** {{Interlanguage link multi|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup}} == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In {{ill|1859|ja|1859年}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年}}), with the opening of {{ill|Yokohama Port|ja|横浜港}}, the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園}}).
In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), along with the relocation of the {{ill|Yūkaku|ja|遊廓}}, the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In {{ill|1882|ja|1882年}} ({{ill|Meiji 15|ja|明治15年}}), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} on September 1, {{ill|1923|ja|1923年}} ({{ill|Taishō 12|ja|大正12年}}) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲}} on May 29, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 20|ja|昭和20年}}). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} ({{ill|Shōwa 63|ja|昭和63年}}). ; Ōtori Shrine In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}}), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}}
