Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=original page at [[:en:draft:Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:35, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohira & Ōtori Jinjja.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|26|13|N|139|37|43|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Minami-ku, Yokohama|Minami-ku (Yokohama)]], {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|qq=}} 1-3 | coordinates = {{ill|coordinates|ja|ウィキ座標|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/coordinates|qq=}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]], {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|3=鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} | shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|qq=}} | established = [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|qq=}}) | main_hall = | other_names = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | annual_festival = | rituals = }} '''Kotohira Ōtori Shrine''' (金刀比羅・大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|qq=}}, [[:en:Minami-ku, Yokohama|Minami-ku (Yokohama)]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|qq=}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari-taisha|qq=}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ** [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] * Ōtori Shrine ** {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|qq=}}), with the opening of [[:en:Port of Yokohama|Yokohama Port]], the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|qq=}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|qq=}} from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Town|qq=}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Park|qq=}}).
In [[:en:1872|1872]] ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 5|qq=}}), along with the relocation of the [[:en:Yūkaku|Yūkaku]], the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In [[:en:1882|1882]] ({{ill|Meiji 15|ja|明治15年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 15|qq=}}), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|qq=}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}} on September 1, [[:en:1923|1923]] ({{ill|Taishō 12|ja|大正12年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō 12|qq=}}) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Air Raid|qq=}} on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] ({{ill|Shōwa 20|ja|昭和20年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 20|qq=}}). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:1988|1988]] ({{ill|Shōwa 63|ja|昭和63年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 63|qq=}}). ; Ōtori Shrine In [[:en:1872|1872]] ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 5|qq=}}), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|qq=}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|qq=}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Ōtori Faith}} {{shinto-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|qq=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|version=102876456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[every:Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[sn:onpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[sn:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧無格社]]

It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari-taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ** [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] * Ōtori Shrine ** {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), with the opening of [[:en:Port of Yokohama|Yokohama Port]], the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Town|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Park|qq=}}).
In [[:en:1872|1872]] ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 5|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1872年}}), along with the relocation of the [[:en:Yūkaku|Yūkaku]], the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In [[:en:1882|1882]] ({{ill|Meiji 15|ja|明治15年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 15|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1882年}}), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|qq=}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}} on September 1, [[:en:1923|1923]] ({{ill|Taishō 12|ja|大正12年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō 12|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1923年}}) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Air Raid|qq=}} on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] ({{ill|Shōwa 20|ja|昭和20年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 20|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1945年}}). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:1988|1988]] ({{ill|Shōwa 63|ja|昭和63年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 63|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1988年}}). ; Ōtori Shrine In [[:en:1872|1872]] ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 5|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1872年}}), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|qq=}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari-taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ** [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] * Ōtori Shrine ** {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), with the opening of [[:en:Port of Yokohama|Yokohama Port]], the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Town|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Park|qq=}}).
In [[:en:1872|1872]] ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 5|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1872年}}), along with the relocation of the [[:en:Yūkaku|Yūkaku]], the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In [[:en:1882|1882]] ({{ill|Meiji 15|ja|明治15年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 15|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1882年}}), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|qq=}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}} on September 1, [[:en:1923|1923]] ({{ill|Taishō 12|ja|大正12年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō 12|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1923年}}) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Air Raid|qq=}} on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] ({{ill|Shōwa 20|ja|昭和20年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 20|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1945年}}). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:1988|1988]] ({{ill|Shōwa 63|ja|昭和63年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 63|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1988年}}). ; Ōtori Shrine In [[:en:1872|1872]] ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 5|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1872年}}), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|qq=}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Ōtori Faith}} {{shinto-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|qq=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|version=102876456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[every:Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[sn:onpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[sn:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧無格社]]

It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari-taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ** [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] * Ōtori Shrine ** {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), with the opening of [[:en:Port of Yokohama|Yokohama Port]], the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Town|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Park|qq=}}).
In [[:en:1872|1872]] ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 5|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1872年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]), along with the relocation of the [[:en:Yūkaku|Yūkaku]], the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In [[:en:1882|1882]] ({{ill|Meiji 15|ja|明治15年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 15|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1882年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|qq=}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}} on September 1, [[:en:1923|1923]] ({{ill|Taishō 12|ja|大正12年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō 12|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1923年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Air Raid|qq=}} on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] ({{ill|Shōwa 20|ja|昭和20年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 20|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1945年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:1988|1988]] ({{ill|Shōwa 63|ja|昭和63年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 63|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1988年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]). ; Ōtori Shrine In [[:en:1872|1872]] ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 5|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1872年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|qq=}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Ōtori Faith}} {{shinto-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|qq=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|version=102876456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[every:Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[sn:onpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[sn:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧無格社]]

The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari-taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ** [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] * Ōtori Shrine ** {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), with the opening of [[:en:Port of Yokohama|Yokohama Port]], the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Town|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Park|qq=}}).
In [[:en:1872|1872]] ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja||1872年|1=1872|2=ja|3=1872年|lt=Meiji 5|lt_ja=明治5年||qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 5|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1872年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]), along with the relocation of the [[:en:Yūkaku|Yūkaku]], the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In [[:en:1882|1882]] ({{ill|Meiji 15|ja||1882年|1=1882|2=ja|3=1882年|lt=Meiji 15|lt_ja=明治15年||qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 15|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1882年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|qq=}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}} on September 1, [[:en:1923|1923]] ({{ill|Taishō 12|ja||1923年|1=1923|2=ja|3=1923年|lt=Taisho 11|lt_ja=大正11年||qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō 12|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1923年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Air Raid|qq=}} on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] ({{ill|Shōwa 20|ja||1945年|1=1945|2=ja|3=1945年|lt=Showa 20|lt_ja=昭和20年||qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 20|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1945年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:1988|1988]] ({{ill|Shōwa 63|ja||1988年|1=1988|2=ja|3=1988年|lt=Showa 63|lt_ja=昭和63年||qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 63|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1988年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]). ; Ōtori Shrine In [[:en:1872|1872]] ({{ill|Meiji 5|ja||1872年|1=1872|2=ja|3=1872年|lt=Meiji 5|lt_ja=明治5年||qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 5|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1872年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|qq=}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Ōtori Faith}} {{shinto-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|qq=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|version=102876456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[every:Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[sn:onpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[sn:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧無格社]]

Torii and Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|26|13|N|139|37|43|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Minami-ku, Yokohama|Minami-ku (Yokohama)]], {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|qq=}} 1-3 | coordinates = {{ill|coordinates|ja|ウィキ座標|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/coordinates|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]], {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|3=鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} | shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | established = [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | main_hall = | other_names = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | annual_festival = | rituals = }} '''Kotohira Ōtori Shrine''' (金刀比羅・大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|qq=}}, [[:en:Minami-ku, Yokohama|Minami-ku (Yokohama)]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari-taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ** [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] * Ōtori Shrine ** {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), with the opening of [[:en:Port of Yokohama|Yokohama Port]], the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Town|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Park|qq=}}).
In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]), along with the relocation of the [[:en:Yūkaku|Yūkaku]], the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In [[:en:1882|1882]] ([[:en:1882|Meiji 15]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|qq=}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}} on September 1, [[:en:1923|1923]] ([[:en:1923|Taisho 11]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Air Raid|qq=}} on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] ([[:en:1945|Showa 20]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:1988|1988]] ([[:en:1988|Showa 63]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]). ; Ōtori Shrine In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|qq=}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari-taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ** [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] * Ōtori Shrine ** {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), with the opening of [[:en:Port of Yokohama|Yokohama Port]], the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Town|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Park|qq=}}).
In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]), along with the relocation of the [[:en:Yūkaku|Yūkaku]], the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In [[:en:1882|1882]] ([[:en:1882|Meiji 15]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|qq=}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}} on September 1, [[:en:1923|1923]] ([[:en:1923|Taisho 11]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Air Raid|qq=}} on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] ([[:en:1945|Showa 20]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:1988|1988]] ([[:en:1988|Showa 63]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]). ; Ōtori Shrine In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|qq=}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Ōtori Faith}} {{shinto-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|qq=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|version=102876456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[every:Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[sn:onpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[sn:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧無格社]]

The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari-taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ** [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] * Ōtori Shrine ** {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), with the opening of [[:en:Port of Yokohama|Yokohama Port]], the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]), along with the relocation of the [[:en:Yūkaku|Yūkaku]], the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In [[:en:1882|1882]] ([[:en:1882|Meiji 15]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|qq=}} for the same reasons.

Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:1988|1988]] ([[:en:1988|Showa 63]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]). ; Ōtori Shrine In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|qq=}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari-taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ** [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] * Ōtori Shrine ** {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), with the opening of [[:en:Port of Yokohama|Yokohama Port]], the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Town|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Park|qq=}}).
In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]), along with the relocation of the [[:en:Yūkaku|Yūkaku]], the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In [[:en:1882|1882]] ([[:en:1882|Meiji 15]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|qq=}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}} on September 1, [[:en:1923|1923]] ([[:en:1923|Taisho 11]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Air Raid|qq=}} on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] ([[:en:1945|Showa 20]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:1988|1988]] ([[:en:1988|Showa 63]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]). ; Ōtori Shrine In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|qq=}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Ōtori Faith}} {{shinto-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|qq=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|version=102876456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[every:Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[sn:onpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[sn:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]]

The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari-taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ** [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] * Ōtori Shrine ** {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), with the opening of [[:en:Port of Yokohama|Yokohama Port]], the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]), along with the relocation of the [[:en:Yūkaku|Yūkaku]], the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In [[:en:1882|1882]] ([[:en:1882|Meiji 15]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|qq=}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}} on September 1, [[:en:1923|1923]] ([[:en:1923|Taisho 11]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Air Raid|qq=}} on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] ([[:en:1945|Showa 20]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:1988|1988]] ([[:en:1988|Showa 63]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]). ; Ōtori Shrine In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|qq=}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari-taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ** [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] * Ōtori Shrine ** {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), with the opening of [[:en:Port of Yokohama|Yokohama Port]], the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Town|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Park|qq=}}).
In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]]), along with the relocation of the [[:en:Yūkaku|Yūkaku]], the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In [[:en:1882|1882]] ([[:en:1882|Meiji 15]]), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|qq=}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}} on September 1, [[:en:1923|1923]] ([[:en:1923|Taisho 11]]) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Air Raid|qq=}} on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] ([[:en:1945|Showa 20]]). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:1988|1988]] ([[:en:1988|Showa 63]]). ; Ōtori Shrine In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]]), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|qq=}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Ōtori Faith}} {{shinto-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|qq=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|version=102876456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[every:Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[sn:onpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[sn:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]]

The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari-taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ** [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] * Ōtori Shrine ** {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), with the opening of [[:en:Port of Yokohama|Yokohama Port]], the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Town|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Park|qq=}}).
In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]]), along with the relocation of the [[:en:Yūkaku|Yūkaku]], the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In [[:en:1882|1882]] ([[:en:1882|Meiji 15]]), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|qq=}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}} on September 1, [[:en:1923|1923]] ([[:en:1923|Taisho 11]]) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Air Raid|qq=}} on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] ([[:en:1945|Showa 20]]). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:1988|1988]] ([[:en:1988|Showa 63]]). ; Ōtori Shrine In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]]), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|qq=}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Ōtori Faith}} {{shinto-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|qq=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|version=102876456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[every:Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[sn:onpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[sn:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

In [[:en:1882|1882]] ([[:en:1882|Meiji 15]]), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|qq=}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}} on September 1, [[:en:1923|1923]] ([[:en:1923|Taisho 11]]) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Air Raid|qq=}} on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] ([[:en:1945|Showa 20]]). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:1988|1988]] ([[:en:1988|Showa 63]]). ; Ōtori Shrine In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]]), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|qq=}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Ōtori Faith}} {{shinto-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|qq=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|version=102876456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[every:Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[sn:onpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[sn:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

[[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Minami-ku, Yokohama|Minami-ku (Yokohama)]], {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]], {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|3=鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} | shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | main_hall = | other_names = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | annual_festival = | rituals = | location = {{ill|Minami-ku|qid=Q1153982}} }} '''Kotohira Ōtori Shrine''' (金刀比羅・大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|qq=|1=Maganechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583588}}, [[:en:Minami-ku, Yokohama|Minami-ku (Yokohama)]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari-taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ** [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] * Ōtori Shrine ** {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), with the opening of [[:en:Port of Yokohama|Yokohama Port]], the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563079}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Town|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Park|qq=|1=Yokohama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542924}}).
In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]]), along with the relocation of the [[:en:Yūkaku|Yūkaku]], the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In [[:en:1882|1882]] ([[:en:1882|Meiji 15]]), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|qq=|1=Maganechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583588}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} on September 1, [[:en:1923|1923]] ([[:en:1923|Taisho 11]]) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Air Raid|qq=|1=Bombing of Yokohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542960}} on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] ([[:en:1945|Showa 20]]). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:1988|1988]] ([[:en:1988|Showa 63]]). ; Ōtori Shrine In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]]), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563079}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|qq=|1=Yoshihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4053786}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Ōtori Faith}} {{shinto-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|qq=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|version=102876456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[every:Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[sn:onpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[sn:Konpira and Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari-taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ** [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] * Ōtori Shrine ** {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), with the opening of [[:en:Port of Yokohama|Yokohama Port]], the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563079}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]].
