(UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kotohira & Ōtori Jinjja.jpg | location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Minami-ku, Yokohama|Minami-ku (Yokohama)]], {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|1=Maganechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583588}} 1-3 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.436944|N|139.628611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]], {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|3=鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} | shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | main_hall = | other_names = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | annual_festival = | rituals = | location = {{ill|Minami-ku|WD=Q1153982}} }} '''Kotohira Ōtori Shrine''' (金刀比羅・大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|1=Maganechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583588}}, [[:en:Minami-ku, Yokohama|Minami-ku (Yokohama)]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ** [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] * Ōtori Shrine ** {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), with the opening of [[:en:Port of Yokohama|Yokohama Port]], the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563079}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Park|1=Yokohama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542924}}).
In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]]), along with the relocation of the [[:en:Yūkaku|Yūkaku]], the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In [[:en:1882|1882]] ([[:en:1882|Meiji 15]]), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|1=Maganechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583588}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} on September 1, [[:en:1923|1923]] ([[:en:1923|Taisho 11]]) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Air Raid|1=Bombing of Yokohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542960}} on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] ([[:en:1945|Showa 20]]). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:1988|1988]] ([[:en:1988|Showa 63]]). ; Ōtori Shrine In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]]), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563079}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|1=Yoshihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4053786}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Ōtori Faith}} {{shinto-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|qq=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|version=102876456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine}} [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11646483}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:1988|1988]] ([[:en:1988|Showa 63]]). ; Ōtori Shrine In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]]), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563079}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|1=Yoshihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4053786}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Ōtori Faith}} {{shinto-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|qq=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|version=102876456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine}} [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11646483}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

location = {{ill|Minami-ku|WD=Q1153982}} }} '''Kotohira Ōtori Shrine''' (金刀比羅・大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|1=Maganechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583588}}, [[:en:Minami-ku, Yokohama|Minami-ku (Yokohama)]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ** [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] * Ōtori Shrine ** {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), with the opening of [[:en:Port of Yokohama|Yokohama Port]], the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563079}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Park|1=Yokohama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542924}}).
In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]]), along with the relocation of the [[:en:Yūkaku|Yūkaku]], the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In [[:en:1882|1882]] ([[:en:1882|Meiji 15]]), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|1=Maganechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583588}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} on September 1, [[:en:1923|1923]] ([[:en:1923|Taisho 11]]) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Air Raid|1=Bombing of Yokohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542960}} on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] ([[:en:1945|Showa 20]]). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:1988|1988]] ([[:en:1988|Showa 63]]). ; Ōtori Shrine In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]]), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563079}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|1=Yoshihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4053786}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:1988|1988]] ([[:en:1988|Showa 63]]). ; Ōtori Shrine In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]]), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563079}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|1=Yoshihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4053786}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Ōtori Faith}} {{shinto-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|qq=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|version=102876456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine}} [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11646483}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Town|1=Maganechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583588}}, [[:en:Minami-ku, Yokohama|Minami-ku (Yokohama)]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ** [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] * Ōtori Shrine ** {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), with the opening of [[:en:Port of Yokohama|Yokohama Port]], the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563079}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Park|1=Yokohama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542924}}).
In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]]), along with the relocation of the [[:en:Yūkaku|Yūkaku]], the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In [[:en:1882|1882]] ([[:en:1882|Meiji 15]]), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|1=Maganechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583588}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} on September 1, [[:en:1923|1923]] ([[:en:1923|Taisho 11]]) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Air Raid|1=Bombing of Yokohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542960}} on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] ([[:en:1945|Showa 20]]). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:1988|1988]] ([[:en:1988|Showa 63]]). ; Ōtori Shrine In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]]), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563079}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|1=Yoshihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4053786}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. In [[:en:1882|1882]] ([[:en:1882|Meiji 15]]), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|1=Maganechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583588}} for the same reasons.

Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:1988|1988]] ([[:en:1988|Showa 63]]). ; Ōtori Shrine In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]]), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563079}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|1=Yoshihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4053786}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Ōtori Faith}} {{shinto-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|qq=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|version=102876456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine}} [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11646483}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11646483]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=original page at [[:en:draft:Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:35, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kotohira & Ōtori Jinjja.jpg | location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Minami-ku, Yokohama|Minami-ku (Yokohama)]], {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|1=Maganechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583588}} 1-3 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.436944|N|139.628611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]], {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|3=鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} | shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | main_hall = | other_names = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | annual_festival = | rituals = | location = {{ill|Minami-ku|WD=Q1153982}} }} '''Kotohira Ōtori Shrine''' (金刀比羅・大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|1=Maganechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583588}}, [[:en:Minami-ku, Yokohama|Minami-ku (Yokohama)]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ** [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] * Ōtori Shrine ** {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), with the opening of [[:en:Port of Yokohama|Yokohama Port]], the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563079}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Park|1=Yokohama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542924}}).
In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]]), along with the relocation of the [[:en:Yūkaku|Yūkaku]], the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In [[:en:1882|1882]] ([[:en:1882|Meiji 15]]), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|1=Maganechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583588}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} on September 1, [[:en:1923|1923]] ([[:en:1923|Taisho 11]]) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Air Raid|1=Bombing of Yokohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542960}} on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] ([[:en:1945|Showa 20]]). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:1988|1988]] ([[:en:1988|Showa 63]]). ; Ōtori Shrine In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]]), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563079}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|1=Yoshihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4053786}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Ōtori Faith}} {{shinto-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|qq=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|version=102876456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine}} [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11646483}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ** [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] * Ōtori Shrine ** {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), with the opening of [[:en:Port of Yokohama|Yokohama Port]], the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563079}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. In [[:en:1882|1882]] ([[:en:1882|Meiji 15]]), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|1=Maganechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583588}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} on September 1, [[:en:1923|1923]] ([[:en:1923|Taisho 11]]) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Air Raid|1=Bombing of Yokohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542960}} on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] ([[:en:1945|Showa 20]]). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:1988|1988]] ([[:en:1988|Showa 63]]). ; Ōtori Shrine In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]]), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563079}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|1=Yoshihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4053786}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Ōtori Faith}} {{shinto-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|qq=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|version=102876456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine}} [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11646483}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

(UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kotohira & Ōtori Jinjja.jpg | location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Minami-ku, Yokohama|Minami-ku (Yokohama)]], {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|1=Maganechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583588}} 1-3 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.436944|N|139.628611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]], {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|3=鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} | shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | main_hall = | other_names = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | annual_festival = | rituals = | location = {{ill|Minami-ku|WD=Q1153982}} }} '''Kotohira Ōtori Shrine''' (金刀比羅・大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|1=Maganechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583588}}, [[:en:Minami-ku, Yokohama|Minami-ku (Yokohama)]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ** [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] * Ōtori Shrine ** {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), with the opening of [[:en:Port of Yokohama|Yokohama Port]], the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563079}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Park|1=Yokohama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542924}}).
In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]]), along with the relocation of the [[:en:Yūkaku|Yūkaku]], the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In [[:en:1882|1882]] ([[:en:1882|Meiji 15]]), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|1=Maganechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583588}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} on September 1, [[:en:1923|1923]] ([[:en:1923|Taisho 11]]) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Air Raid|1=Bombing of Yokohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542960}} on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] ([[:en:1945|Showa 20]]). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:1988|1988]] ([[:en:1988|Showa 63]]). ; Ōtori Shrine In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]]), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563079}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|1=Yoshihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4053786}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Ōtori Faith}} {{shinto-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|qq=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|version=102876456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine}} [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11646483}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ** [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] * Ōtori Shrine ** {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), with the opening of [[:en:Port of Yokohama|Yokohama Port]], the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563079}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]]), along with the relocation of the [[:en:Yūkaku|Yūkaku]], the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In [[:en:1882|1882]] ([[:en:1882|Meiji 15]]), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|1=Maganechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583588}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} on September 1, [[:en:1923|1923]] ([[:en:1923|Taisho 11]]) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Air Raid|1=Bombing of Yokohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542960}} on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] ([[:en:1945|Showa 20]]). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:1988|1988]] ([[:en:1988|Showa 63]]). ; Ōtori Shrine In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]]), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563079}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|1=Yoshihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4053786}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. location = {{ill|Minami-ku|WD=Q1153982}} }} '''Kotohira Ōtori Shrine''' (金刀比羅・大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|1=Maganechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583588}}, [[:en:Minami-ku, Yokohama|Minami-ku (Yokohama)]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ** [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] * Ōtori Shrine ** {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), with the opening of [[:en:Port of Yokohama|Yokohama Port]], the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563079}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Park|1=Yokohama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542924}}).
In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]]), along with the relocation of the [[:en:Yūkaku|Yūkaku]], the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In [[:en:1882|1882]] ([[:en:1882|Meiji 15]]), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|1=Maganechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583588}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} on September 1, [[:en:1923|1923]] ([[:en:1923|Taisho 11]]) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Air Raid|1=Bombing of Yokohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542960}} on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] ([[:en:1945|Showa 20]]). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:1988|1988]] ([[:en:1988|Showa 63]]). ; Ōtori Shrine In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]]), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563079}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|1=Yoshihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4053786}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. In [[:en:1882|1882]] ([[:en:1882|Meiji 15]]), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|1=Maganechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583588}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} on September 1, [[:en:1923|1923]] ([[:en:1923|Taisho 11]]) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Air Raid|1=Bombing of Yokohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542960}} on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] ([[:en:1945|Showa 20]]). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:1988|1988]] ([[:en:1988|Showa 63]]). ; Ōtori Shrine In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]]), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563079}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|1=Yoshihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4053786}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Ōtori Faith}} {{shinto-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|qq=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|version=102876456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} {{everybodywiki link|Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11646483}} [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

(UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kotohira & Ōtori Jinjja.jpg | location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Minami-ku, Yokohama|Minami-ku (Yokohama)]], {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|1=Maganechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583588}} 1-3 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.436944|N|139.628611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]], {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|3=鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} | shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | main_hall = | other_names = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | annual_festival = | rituals = | location = {{ill|Minami-ku|WD=Q1153982}} }} '''Kotohira Ōtori Shrine''' (金刀比羅・大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|1=Maganechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583588}}, [[:en:Minami-ku, Yokohama|Minami-ku (Yokohama)]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ** [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] * Ōtori Shrine ** {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In [[:en:1859|1859]] ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), with the opening of [[:en:Port of Yokohama|Yokohama Port]], the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563079}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Park|1=Yokohama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542924}}).
In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]]), along with the relocation of the [[:en:Yūkaku|Yūkaku]], the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In [[:en:1882|1882]] ([[:en:1882|Meiji 15]]), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|1=Maganechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583588}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} on September 1, [[:en:1923|1923]] ([[:en:1923|Taisho 11]]) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Air Raid|1=Bombing of Yokohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542960}} on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] ([[:en:1945|Showa 20]]). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:1988|1988]] ([[:en:1988|Showa 63]]). ; Ōtori Shrine In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:1872|Meiji 5]]), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563079}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|1=Yoshihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4053786}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Ōtori Faith}} {{shinto-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|qq=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|version=102876456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{everybodywiki link|Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11646483}} [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

In {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} ({{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5}}), along with the relocation of the {{ill|Yūkaku|en|Yūkaku|WD=Q1060846|lt=Yūkaku}}, the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In {{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=1882}} ({{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=Meiji 15}}), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|1=Maganechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583588}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} on September 1, {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=1923}} ({{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=Taisho 11}}) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Air Raid|1=Bombing of Yokohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542960}} on May 29, {{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945}} ({{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=Showa 20}}). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|1988|en|1988|WD=Q2426|lt=1988}} ({{ill|1988|en|1988|WD=Q2426|lt=Showa 63}}). ; Ōtori Shrine In {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} ({{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5}}), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563079}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|1=Yoshihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4053786}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Ōtori Faith}} {{shinto-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|qq=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|version=102876456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{everybodywiki link|Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11646483}} [[Category:Buildings in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
