redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]), {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|WD=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku|1=Emperor Sutoku}}, {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|3=鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} | shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked shrines|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = {{ill|1859|en|1859|WD=Q7687|lt=1859|1=1859}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | main_hall = | other_names = Kotohira Ōtori Shrine | annual_festival = | rituals = | location = {{ill|Minami-ku|WD=Q1153982|1=Minami-ku, Yokohama}} }} '''Kotohira Ōtori Shrine''' (金刀比羅・大鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|1=Maganechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583588}}, {{ill|Minami-ku, Yokohama|en|Minami-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1153982|lt=Minami-ku (Yokohama)|1=Minami-ku, Yokohama}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama|1=Yokohama}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is a former {{ill|Unranked Shrine|ja|無格社|1=Unranked shrines|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unranked Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The registered name as a religious corporation is '''Religious Corporation Kotohira Shrine''' (宗教法人金刀比羅神社). The shrine grounds also include a subordinate shrine dedicated to {{ill|Fushimi Inari Shrine|ja|伏見稲荷神社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社}}. == Deities == * Kotohira Shrine ** {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ** {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|WD=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku|1=Emperor Sutoku}} * Ōtori Shrine ** {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] == History == ; Kotohira Shrine In {{ill|1859|en|1859|WD=Q7687|lt=1859|1=1859}} ({{ill|Ansei 6|ja|安政6年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ansei 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), with the opening of {{ill|Port of Yokohama|en|Port of Yokohama|WD=Q1190021|lt=Yokohama Port|1=Port of Yokohama}}, the proprietor of the Iwakame Rō brothel in the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563079}}, Sakichi of Iwatsukiya, enshrined the {{ill|Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja|象頭山 金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōzu-san Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province|1=Sanuki Province}}. The shrine was initially established in the {{ill|Minatozaki Town|ja|港崎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} area (the current site of {{ill|Yokohama Park|ja|横浜公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Park|1=Yokohama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542924}}).
In {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872|1=1872}} ({{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5|1=1872}}), along with the relocation of the {{ill|Yūkaku|en|Yūkaku|WD=Q1060846|lt=Yūkaku|1=Yūkaku}}, the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In {{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=1882|1=1882}} ({{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=Meiji 15|1=1882}}), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|1=Maganechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583588}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} on September 1, {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=1923|1=1923}} ({{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=Taisho 11|1=1923}}) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Air Raid|1=Bombing of Yokohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542960}} on May 29, {{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}} ({{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=Showa 20|1=1945}}). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|1988|en|1988|WD=Q2426|lt=1988|1=1988}} ({{ill|1988|en|1988|WD=Q2426|lt=Showa 63|1=1988}}). ; Ōtori Shrine In {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872|1=1872}} ({{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5|1=1872}}), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563079}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|1=Yoshihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4053786}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo Proper|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Ōtori Faith}} {{shinto-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|qq=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|version=102876456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{everybodywiki link|Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11646483}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

In {{ill|1872|en|1872|qid=Q7753|lt=1872|1=1872}} ({{ill|1872|en|1872|qid=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5|1=1872}}), along with the relocation of the {{ill|Yūkaku|en|Yūkaku|qid=Q1060846|lt=Yūkaku|1=Yūkaku}}, the shrine was moved to the coastal area of Takashima Town 7-chōme and renamed Kotohira Shrine. In {{ill|1882|en|1882|qid=Q7812|lt=1882|1=1882}} ({{ill|1882|en|1882|qid=Q7812|lt=Meiji 15|1=1882}}), it was relocated again to {{ill|Magane Town|ja|真金町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magane Town|1=Maganechō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11583588}} for the same reasons.
The shrine was damaged during both the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3742341}} on September 1, {{ill|1923|en|1923|qid=Q2169|lt=1923|1=1923}} ({{ill|1923|en|1923|qid=Q2169|lt=Taisho 11|1=1923}}) and the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Air Raid|1=Bombing of Yokohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11542960}} on May 29, {{ill|1945|en|1945|qid=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}} ({{ill|1945|en|1945|qid=Q5240|lt=Showa 20|1=1945}}). Although it was rebuilt each time, the current main shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|1988|en|1988|qid=Q2426|lt=1988|1=1988}} ({{ill|1988|en|1988|qid=Q2426|lt=Showa 63|1=1988}}). ; Ōtori Shrine In {{ill|1872|en|1872|qid=Q7753|lt=1872|1=1872}} ({{ill|1872|en|1872|qid=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5|1=1872}}), when the {{ill|Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja|港崎遊郭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatozaki Yūkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 港崎遊廓|1=Miyozaki Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11563079}} was moved to Takashima Town, a deity known as the "Otorisama" was enshrined following the example of {{ill|Yoshiwara Yūkaku|ja|吉原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Yūkaku|1=Yoshihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4053786}} in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo Proper|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q7473516}}.It is unclear whether this refers to {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} in Asakusa, Tokyo, or {{ill|Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Adachi)|qq=}} in Hanahata, Adachi, Tokyo. From this time, the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival was held, similar to Asakusa. Initially, it was a subordinate shrine within the grounds, but it is now enshrined alongside Kotohira Shrine. == location == * 1-3 Magane Town, Minami-ku, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://yokohama-torinoichi.jimdofree.com/ Kotohira Ōtori Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Ōtori Faith}} {{shinto-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|qq=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|version=102876456|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{everybodywiki link|Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine}} [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] [[ja:金刀比羅・大鷲神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11646483}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Minami-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
